questions.question: Which technology is characterized by its use of both p-type and n-type MOSFETs to create logic gates, enabling low static power consumption in modern digital integrated circuits?
In the context of computer architecture, what is the primary role of a Branch Predictor unit within a modern CPU pipeline?
options: To reduce the latency of memory access by prefetching data.
To optimize the execution of arithmetic and logical operations.
To guess the outcome of conditional branch instructions to avoid pipeline stalls.
To manage the allocation and deallocation of registers for out-of-order execution.
To ensure cache coherence across multiple processor cores.

questions.question: When designing digital circuits, what phenomenon occurs when the clock signal arrives at different flip-flops at slightly different times across the chip, potentially leading to setup or hold time violations if not properly managed?

In the physical design of digital integrated circuits, particularly at advanced technology nodes, what is the dominant factor contributing to signal propagation delay in long interconnect wires, especially as compared to gate delay?
options: Process variation in transistor threshold voltage

questions.question: Which digital IC design methodology relies heavily on the reuse of pre-designed, pre-verified intellectual property (IP) blocks (e.g., CPU cores, DSPs, memory controllers) to rapidly construct complex System-on-Chip (SoC) designs?
